citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
During recordkeeping review, a deviation from an approved IACUC study protocol was observed: the euthanasia
procedure for cardiac perfusion under gas anesthesia indicates the animals will receive additional analgesia in the form of
opioid injection after sedation and induction. Twelve cynomolgus macaques that were euthanized via perfusion on 12
January 2026 and 16 January 2026 (6 animals on each day) were not administered the listed opioids of either
hydromorphone or morphine. A member of the veterinary care team stated that both drugs have not been on site and no
controlled drug logs were available for review. The staff member stated there were no adverse events observed during
the euthanasia perfusion procedure.
This activity constitutes a significant deviation from the approved activities in the protocol. Making significant changes to
protocols without IACUC review and approval deprives the IACUC of the opportunity to provide oversight of animal
activities and compliance with the Animal Welfare Act.
Principal investigators are to follow the IACUC approved protocols, and any proposed changes shall be reviewed and
approved by the IACUC prior to implementation. Those carrying out procedures in an IACUC-approved protocol must
adhere to what was approved by the IACUC.
Correct by 30 April 2026.

The last two review dates of the contingency plan listed are 20 March 2026 and 14 August 2023, creating a review
interval of greater than 1 year. Failure to review the contingency plan on at least an annual basis may result in outdated
information and action plans that may negatively impact animal welfare during an emergency. The contingency plan must
be reviewed by the research facility on at least an annual basis to ensure that it adequately addresses the criteria listed in
paragraph (l)(1) and maintain annual review documentation. Contingency plans, as well as all annual review
documentation, must be made available to APHIS upon request.
Correct from this date forward of 30 March 2026.

The metal food receptacles used for the marmosets that are stored in the common area are only cleaned and not
sanitized between use according to facility representatives. The facility uses dish soap and water to clean the food
receptacles. Not sanitizing food receptacles as described in this subpart may increase risk of disease transmission
between animals.
Food receptacles must be kept clean and must be sanitized in accordance with the procedures listed in § 3.84(b)(3) of
this subpart at least once every 2 weeks. Used food receptacles must be sanitized before they can be used to provide
food to a different nonhuman primate or social grouping of nonhuman primates. Measures must be taken to ensure there
is no molding, deterioration, contamination, or caking or wetting of food placed in self-feeders.
Correct by 31 March 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(7) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
During recordkeeping review, a deviation from an approved IACUC study protocol was observed: the euthanasia 
procedure for cardiac perfusion under gas anesthesia indicates the animals will receive additional analgesia in the form of 
opioid injection after sedation and induction. Twelve cynomolgus macaques that were euthanized via perfusion on 12 
January 2026 and 16 January 2026 (6 animals on each day) were not administered the listed opioids of either 
hydromorphone or morphine. A member of the veterinary care team stated that both drugs have not been on site and no 
controlled drug logs were available for review. The staff member stated there were no adverse events observed during 
the euthanasia perfusion procedure. 
 
This activity constitutes a significant deviation from the approved activities in the protocol. Making significant changes to 
protocols without IACUC review and approval deprives the IACUC of the opportunity to provide oversight of animal 
activities and compliance with the Animal Welfare Act.  
 
Principal investigators are to follow the IACUC approved protocols, and any proposed changes shall be reviewed and 
approved by the IACUC prior to implementation. Those carrying out procedures in an IACUC-approved protocol must 
adhere to what was approved by the IACUC. 
 
Correct by 30 April 2026. 
2.38(l)(2) 
   
Miscellaneous. 
The last two review dates of the contingency plan listed are 20 March 2026 and 14 August 2023, creating a review 
interval of greater than 1 year. Failure to review the contingency plan on at least an annual basis may result in outdated 
information and action plans that may negatively impact animal welfare during an emergency. The contingency plan must 
be reviewed by the research facility on at least an annual basis to ensure that it adequately addresses the criteria listed in 
paragraph (l)(1) and maintain annual review documentation. Contingency plans, as well as all annual review 
documentation, must be made available to APHIS upon request.

Correct from this date forward of 30 March 2026. 
3.82(d) 
   
Feeding. 
The metal food receptacles used for the marmosets that are stored in the common area are only cleaned and not 
sanitized between use according to facility representatives. The facility uses dish soap and water to clean the food 
receptacles. Not sanitizing food receptacles as described in this subpart may increase risk of disease transmission 
between animals. 
 
Food receptacles must be kept clean and must be sanitized in accordance with the procedures listed in § 3.84(b)(3) of 
this subpart at least once every 2 weeks. Used food receptacles must be sanitized before they can be used to provide 
food to a different nonhuman primate or social grouping of nonhuman primates. Measures must be taken to ensure there 
is no molding, deterioration, contamination, or caking or wetting of food placed in self-feeders. 
 
Correct by 31 March 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
